items.title: By rejecting his bid for appeal the High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure
items.summary: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

items.title: Lance Collard's extraordinary AFL disciplinary hearing sets new precedent
items.summary: In an AFL first, St Kilda's Lance Collard has faced a disciplinary tribunal hearing for conduct unbecoming.

items.title: Fuel crisis sees less motorists on Sydney roads but small rise in public transport use, data shows
items.summary: It is exactly six weeks since the conflict began in the MIddle East and data suggests Sydney motorists have changed how they travel in order to cope with record fuel prices.

items.title: Cyclone Maila weakening and slowing as it heads towards Australia
items.summary: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

items.title: Hobart City Mission repairing donated clothing to reduce textile waste
items.summary: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

items.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos (Keeling) Islands walked back after backlash
items.summary: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

items.title: Tamworth teen Max Lye is no chicken in the egg business
items.summary: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

items.title: Rotting sperm whale carcass forces relocation of Tasmania's largest open water swim
items.summary: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

items.title: Armidale museum freezes dozens of koalas for taxidermy and research
items.summary: Most people usually drive past animals killed on the road, but in Armidale, they stop, bag them up, and drop them off at the local museum.

items.title: British man who avoided deportation after child sex offence could face fresh visa review
items.summary: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

items.title: Police appeal for information about mysterious shooting death of Richard Wills at Ouyen
items.summary: Richard Wills was found shot and buried in a shallow grave at his rural property in Victoria's north-west earlier this week.

items.title: Astronauts hoisted to recovery ship after 'perfect bullseye' landing — as it happened
items.summary: The crew on board the Orion has returned to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean in a perfect '"bullseye". Read back over the day's event in our Artemis II blog.

items.title: Long-range goals and their impact on the 2026 AFL season
items.summary: In a league where inside-50s are so often king, teams with prowess from outside the arc are making a major impact to start the 2026 AFL season.

items.title: Renewed push for completion of dumped Victorian Murray Basin freight rail project
items.summary: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

items.title: Alex de Minaur suffers painful Monte Carlo quarterfinal loss to Monaco's Valentin Vacherot
items.summary: Alex de Minaur will slip from his career-high ranking after missing a chance to emulate his semifinal run at the Monte Carlo Masters, beaten in three sets by Monaco's Valentin Vacherot.

items.title: What to do if you get a fishhook injury and how to avoid one
items.summary: What do you do when the hook catches you instead of the fish? Charter fishers and a medical expert weigh in.

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase as dairy farmers hit hard by fuel and fertiliser costs
items.summary: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

items.title: Jess Hull and Claudia Hollingsworth speak after the dramatic women's 1,500m final
items.summary: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

items.title: Israel's ambassador concedes Iran's chokehold on Strait of Hormuz was not expected before war
items.summary: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.

items.title: Trump threatens Iran with warships ahead of 'make-or-break' talks — as it happened
items.summary: US Vice-President JD Vance is due to arrive in Pakistan as both sides level accusations of ceasefire breaches.

items.title: Iran uses sea mine threat to 'maintain chokehold' over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

items.summary: As one of the world's largest music festivals gets underway this weekend, a pop group from the Philippines has made history.

items.title: How Australia is navigating an oil market in crisis
items.summary: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

items.title: Debra Adelaide reflects on friendship with Gabrielle Carey in When I Am Sixty-Four
items.summary: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

items.title: Albanese's petro-diplomacy can't overcome the pain of what may lie ahead
items.summary: This is not over. The hard stuff is only just beginning. And it's going to get bumpy, testing the nation's resilience and its political leadership.

items.title: Trump, Netanyahu and how war in Iran is reshaping US politics

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of a classic Aussie poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy
items.summary: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

items.title: St Kilda forward Lance Collard guilty over using homophobic slur

items.title: NT Police investigating death of child found in Palmerston driveway
items.summary: Northern Territory Police are investigating the death of a 3-month-old who was found inside a vehicle parked in the driveway of a Palmerston home on Friday.

items.title: WA Police say bones found on Rottnest Island are historic Indigenous remains

items.title: Australian War Memorial display on Ben Roberts-Smith altered as refurbished sections unveiled
items.summary: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

items.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in frantic finish
items.summary: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval.

items.title: Dunsborough Primary School left scrambling to pay $130k water bill
items.summary: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

items.summary: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

items.title: Ian Brooke to remain in prison for killing foster brother in Broome
items.summary: Ian Brooke was found not guilty of murder due to his mental impairment, but will have to stay in jail because of a lack of suitable mental health care facilities to house him.

items.title: Man jailed for stomping on head of off-duty police officer in Newtown attack
items.summary: Koby Weaver is jailed over his role in what is described as a "brutal" attack which left an off-duty police officer with serious injuries.

items.title: Jury hears Maree Crabtree, on trial for murdering son Jonathan, allegedly claimed she 'wanted to put something in his drink'
items.summary: A Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son allegedly called him a "potato" with "no life" and claimed she "wanted to put something in his drink so he will just not be here", a jury heard.

items.title: Cowboys claim thrilling Queensland derby victory with last-minute try
items.summary: The North Queensland Cowboys have won a thriller at Lang Park, beating the Brisbane Broncos 35-31. Earlier, the Dragons lost their sixth-straight to start the 2026 NRL season.

items.title: Taiwan's opposition leader Cheng Li-wun has met Xi Jinping in China for the first time
items.summary: Taiwan's opposition leader has done something for the first time in a decade, and experts say it could shape relations between Beijing, Taipei and Washington.

items.title: Anthony Albanese and Singapore's prime minister talk trade, fuel in bilateral meeting
items.summary: Australia and Singapore have agreed to make "maximum efforts" to meet each other's fuel and gas needs as the crisis in the Middle East continues, with the prime minister securing an encouraging but not legally binding deal.

items.title: Brazil cricket player Laura Cardoso takes nine wickets in T20 international to set new record
items.summary: Brazilian cricketer Laura Cardoso has set a new record that is going to be tremendously hard to beat.

items.title: Commuters trapped in South Eastern Freeway gridlock blast lack of signage
items.summary: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

items.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions in cash from illegal tobacco sales
items.summary: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

items.title: Blayze James Noble charged with murder of Christine Joyce Diane Marshall Hunter
items.summary: A Sunshine Coast man has been charged with murdering his girlfriend in the Northern Territory. Ahead of his appearance in court, her sister has spoken of her family's heartbreak.

items.title: At the site of an Israeli strike in Beirut, locals say 'there's no Hezbollah here'
items.summary: In a residential area in Beirut an entire building had been razed to the ground and a nut shop was destroyed. The locals told the ABC "there's no Hezbollah here".

items.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67
items.summary: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

items.title: Bribbaree grandmother Nerol Doble's family says there will be 'no forgiveness' for son who set her on fire
items.summary: NSW grandmother Nerol Doble was murdered by her son Shannon James Doble in 2024 after an argument over the use of a jerry can. During a sentencing hearing, family members told the court of the hell his actions have left them in.

items.title: Artemis II crew about to make splash landing on Earth after journey around Moon
items.summary: The Orion space capsule will enter the Earth's atmosphere as a burning fireball, travelling at about 40,000 km/h, splashing down off the coast of southern California. Here's a breakdown of how the spacecraft copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

items.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend
items.summary: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

items.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
items.summary: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

items.title: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands as it moves through PNG towards Far North Queensland
items.summary: Roads and buildings have been destroyed across multiple Pacific islands as slow-moving Tropical Cyclone Maila heads towards Papua New Guinea, with the eye of the storm set to cross over two remote islands early tomorrow.

items.title: Seal sleeping on road causes Mornington Peninsula traffic delays
items.summary: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

items.title: Robert Noel Rogers jailed for life over 2021 murder of Raymond Scopelitis on Sunshine Coast
items.summary: Following a trial held in Brisbane, a jury deliberated for almost two days before returning its verdict.

items.summary: Guy Haymes was left in a vegetative state after an assault during a dispute over $50 in February, 2024.

items.summary: The Bureau of Meteorology says wet weather conditions will improve for AFL Gather Round matches as the weekend progresses, as the premier shares his ongoing bid to keep the event in South Australia.

items.summary: Penrith centre Casey McLean is facing a one-week suspension for a high shot that left Canterbury prop Max King with a broken jaw.

items.summary: Lawyers for the Queensland government have travelled to Canberra to urge the High Court to toss out a reduced sentence for a teen jailed for killing Emma Lovell in 2022.

items.title: Hills Shire Mayor leads calls to simplify governance of Hawkesbury River
items.summary: The Hawkesbury-Nepean River flows through eight NSW council areas and is looked after by 13 agencies, which one mayor says leads to confusion about who is in charge of what.
